syntheticDeclaration.purpose: SYNTHETIC TEST FIXTURE. Exercises emergency-severity alert dominance over ordinary differential ranking, and proves alert wording escalates to a clinician rather than issuing a treatment instruction. All values are invented for schema and safety-test purposes only.

executionBinding.engineRationale: Instability + fever/neutropenia is expressible as a patient-input.schema.json symptoms/CBC input and evaluated by assessPediatricAnemia.

expectedBehavior.requiredSignal: An urgent instability/critical-result condition must outrank explanatory pathway content. Urgent-alert dominance and clinician escalation wording, without treatment instruction.
forbiddenSignals: any alert action string containing a specific drug name, dose, or numeric dosing/transfusion-volume instruction
an emergency-severity presentation (hemodynamic instability + febrile neutropenia) producing zero alerts

expectedTrace.note: Verified by direct execution on 2026-07-19. Both ALERT-001 (instability) and ALERT-009 (fever+neutropenia) fire at emergency severity; all alert action strings direct escalation/pathway use (e.g. 'Use the local febrile-neutropenia/emergency pathway immediately.') and none specify a drug, dose, or transfusion volume.

rollbackSignal: Re-verify by execution if modules/anemia/rules.json, candidates.json, or module.json digests change, or if ALERT-001/003/004/009 conditions or action text are edited.
